2024-12-24.abstract: President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko of Belarus keeps arresting political opponents, but analysts wonder if a string of unusual pardons is a bid to get sanctions eased.

2024-12-24.title: Under Cambodia’s New Leader, Room for Dissent Narrows
2024-12-24.abstract: Hun Manet, who took over as prime minister last year, has followed in the steps of his father, who ran Cambodia for nearly 40 years.

2024-12-24.title: These Students Ousted a Government. Now They’re Rebuilding a Democracy.
2024-12-24.abstract: It is a daunting task for Bangladesh. But an unusual mix of young people and seasoned technocrats are determined to make the most of their opening.

2024-12-24.title: Sorting Fact From Fiction as Fear Engulfs Bangladesh’s Hindus
2024-12-24.abstract: Reports of vandalism and mob violence are real, but wilder accusations emanating from India muddy the picture.

2024-12-24.title: He Inherited a Seat in Britain’s House of Lords. How Will It Feel to Lose It?
2024-12-24.abstract: The U.K. government has pledged to remove hereditary peers from Parliament in 2025. For Lord Cromwell and 87 others, it is a wistful departure.

2024-12-25.title: Gunmen Attack Haiti’s Largest Public Hospital, Killing at Least 3
2024-12-25.abstract: Two journalists and a police officer died and at least seven others were wounded in the shooting before the reopening of a hospital wing in Port-au-Prince.

2024-12-25.title: 21 Dead as Mozambique Erupts in Violence Over Election Ruling
2024-12-25.abstract: A ruling on Monday confirming that Daniel Chapo had won a disputed election has sparked protests and violence.

2024-12-25.title: Released From a Russian Prison, This Activist Got Right to Work
2024-12-25.abstract: Vladimir Kara-Murza, a vehement critic of President Vladimir V. Putin, didn’t want to be part of a prisoner exchange. But he says more exchanges are needed.

2024-12-25.title: In Southern Lebanon, Trauma and Grief Mark a Somber Christmas
2024-12-25.abstract: The ancient Christian community in the coastal city of Tyre has little cause for celebration this year, with many mourning their dead and still rattled by months of Israeli bombardment.

2024-12-25.title: Around the World, Lights and Color Mark the Season
2024-12-25.abstract: Around the world, people found ways to show their holiday spirit.

2024-12-26.title: Hanukkah in Pictures: Keeping the Flame Alive
2024-12-26.abstract: Jews around the globe lit the first of eight candles on Wednesday and came together to celebrate and reflect on their history.

2024-12-26.title: Palestinian Officials Say 5 Journalists Are Killed in Gaza Strike
2024-12-26.abstract: The Israeli military said it had struck a vehicle containing a “terrorist cell” in the Nuseirat area of Gaza.

2024-12-26.title: Still Sounding Young at 85, She Is the Voice of Old Japan
2024-12-26.abstract: Midori Kato is the last original member of the cast of “Sazae-san,” a cartoon series that premiered in 1969 and never quite joined the modern world.

2024-12-26.title: The War Killed Her Dreams. To Survive, She Treated Its Fighters.
2024-12-26.abstract: Trapped in Sudan’s brutal civil war, a young woman chose to work in a clinic on the front line, treating civilians and combatants. She had to navigate suspicion from both sides.

2024-12-26.title: 20 Years Since the Deadly Tsunami in Asia
2024-12-26.abstract: A look back at the horrible loss of life.

2024-12-27.title: South Korean Lawmakers Impeach Acting President as Crisis Deepens
2024-12-27.abstract: The vote was the second major impeachment in two weeks after President Yoon’s ill-fated martial law bid set off turmoil. The country’s currency has plunged.

2024-12-27.title: Two Sailors Die During Sydney to Hobart Yacht Race
2024-12-27.abstract: The men suffered head injuries on their yachts in separate episodes, as participants faced challenging weather conditions.

2024-12-27.title: Syria’s Alawite Minority, Favored by the Assads, Looks Nervously to the Future
2024-12-27.abstract: Amid an outcry for justice and accountability and threats online, a once-dominant group is feeling deep anxiety after the ouster of Syria’s dictator.

2024-12-27.title: Trump Disparaged Africa. So Why Are Some Africans Optimistic?
2024-12-27.abstract: In his first term, Donald Trump denigrated African nations, but leaders there are hopeful his return will bring more investment and less pressure to uphold democracy and human rights.

2024-12-27.title: The Children Who Left Gaza
2024-12-27.abstract: Sixteen young people evacuated from Gaza for treatment of dire injuries. Months later, they face new lives in an unfamiliar country.

2024-12-28.title: Greeks Are Defying an Indoor Smoking Ban, Even After 14 Years
2024-12-28.abstract: The country, which has one of Europe’s highest smoking rates, is also resisting a wider push for outdoor bans.

2024-12-28.title: Our Reporter Returned to Afghanistan, Seeking Lessons and Secrets
Tormentors Change, but Not the Torment
2024-12-28.abstract: Brutally persecuted for years by the military in Myanmar, the Rohingya ethnic minority has now become the target of one of the junta’s most formidable rivals in the country’s civil war.

2024-12-28.title: Has Russia’s Shadow Fleet, Built to Evade Sanctions, Added Sabotage to Its List?
2024-12-28.abstract: Russia has assembled a fleet of hundreds of vessels to covertly ship its oil. With so many ships at sea, the idea of using some to cause havoc may be proving irresistible to the Kremlin.

2024-12-28.title: Shrapnel and Sturgeon: Making Caviar Under the Rockets in Israel
2024-12-28.abstract: The war with Hezbollah has cleared out the north of the country — save for a hardy few and their thousands of prized fish.

2024-12-29.title: South Korea’s Acting President Has Been in Office Only Since Friday
2024-12-29.abstract: After two impeachments of leaders in two weeks, the country is deep into a political crisis.

2024-12-29.title: Despite Attacks, an Underground Church for L.G.B.T.Q. Africans Thrives
2024-12-29.abstract: The congregation in Nairobi, Kenya, has been forced to move to 10 different locations over 10 years, and yet it has survived as a sanctuary in an increasingly hostile environment.

2024-12-29.title: Ignoring Warnings, a Growing Band of Tourists Venture to Afghanistan
2024-12-29.abstract: With the war now over, the Taliban are welcoming foreign travelers, even as governments advise their citizens to stay away.

2024-12-29.title: Behind the Dismantling of Hezbollah: Decades of Israeli Intelligence
2024-12-29.abstract: A Times investigation shows how extensively Israel penetrated the Lebanese militia, closely tracking the group’s commanders and culminating in the assassination of its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.

2024-12-29.title: Boeing 737-800, a Precursor to the 737 Max, Is Used Widely.
2024-12-29.abstract: Nearly 200 airlines use the 737-800, which makes up about 15 percent of the world’s fleet of aircraft.

2024-12-30.title: ‘She Was Almost Home’: Father Mourns South Korea Plane Crash Victim
2024-12-30.abstract: Jeon Je-young was among the hundreds of family members waiting for news from the authorities about Jeju Air Flight 2216, which crashed at Muan International Airport in South Korea.

2024-12-30.title: Lebanon’s Economy Reels From War: ‘We Are Starting From Zero’
2024-12-30.abstract: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah may be suspended, but the damage is immense. “We came out of this war with nothing,” one man said.

2024-12-30.title: Where Is Russia Finding New Soldiers? Wherever It Can.
2024-12-30.abstract: From murder suspects to immigrants to a former Olympic gold medalist, Russia pressures those it thinks should fight in Ukraine.

2024-12-30.title: The Rivers Run Dry and the Lights Go Out: A Warming Nation’s Doom Loop
2024-12-30.abstract: An extraordinary drought has drained Ecuador’s rivers and reservoirs, leading to power outages of up to 14 hours. Some fear this is the beginning of a larger global crisis.

2024-12-30.title: 5 Babies Die From the Cold in Gaza as Temperatures Drop
2024-12-30.abstract: A boy less than 3 weeks old was among the latest to succumb to winter weather, Gazan officials said. Families displaced by Israel’s attacks have little more than tents and tarps to protect them.

2024-12-31.title: South Korea Clears Way to Detain President in Martial Law Inquiry
2024-12-31.abstract: The police are investigating whether President Yoon Suk Yeol tried to lead an insurrection when he declared martial law and plunged the country into crisis.

2024-12-31.title: Migrants Accused of Killing Mexican Immigration Agent
2024-12-31.abstract: Officials said the agent was attacked by a group of migrants at a checkpoint in the northern state of Chihuahua after asking to see their identification papers. At least two migrants were arrested.

2024-12-31.title: Drugs, Scams and Sin: Myanmar’s War Has Made It the Global Crime Capital
2024-12-31.abstract: The chaotic country is now a magnet for criminal syndicates, particularly from China, destabilizing law enforcement across much of Asia.

2024-12-31.title: Syrian Refugees in Germany Are Glad They Can Visit Home. But Just Visit.
2024-12-31.abstract: The more than one million Syrians who fled to Germany have celebrated the end of war in their homeland, but some fear it could mean losing their refugee status.

2024-12-31.title: The Man Who Showed the World South Korea’s Deadly Plane Crash
2024-12-31.abstract: In his own words, a restaurant owner described recording videos of the Jeju Air plane crash as it happened. “Every time I closed my eyes, I kept seeing afterimages of the blast.”
